title: guettarda baracoensis
intro: Guettarda baracoensis, also known as the White Trumpet Tree, is a flowering plant native to Cuba and the Dominican Republic. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its leaves and flowers, which contain flavonoids, terpenes, and other bioactive compounds. Guettarda baracoensis has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which provide health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. The plant's herbal preparations include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to leverage its medicinal properties.
